summary: DOT attributes the Fiscal 2025 rise in traffic-signal repair response time to increasingly heavy storms and flash flooding across the five boroughs, which lengthened travel times for repair crews and added signal repairs. Preliminary Fiscal 2026 data through October 2025 shows response times continuing to run above the two-hour target, though the city has not yet published a specific explanation for the size of that year's increase.

sources.label: NYC Department of Transportation, Fiscal 2025 Mayor's Management Report
NYC Department of Transportation, Fiscal 2026 Preliminary Mayor's Management Report

oneLiner: increasing, which DOT attributes to heavier storms and flash flooding
